texto: The beginning of Eternity, The end of time and space, The beginning of every end, And the end of every place. What am I?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Georgia Byng que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “The beginning of Eternity, The end of time and space, The beginning of every end, And the end of …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, eternity, georgia-byng, com foco em termino e georgia-byng.

frase_original: The beginning of Eternity, The end of time and space, The beginning of every end, And the end of every place. What am I?

texto: Now, we all have stories of how we got here, and prob-probably some of you feel angry who whoever it is who's left you here. But you must try and remember that they were like that because that's how they were taught to be. You m-must try to forgive them. Baby cuckoos can't unlearn their bad habits. But we should try to, and because what you learn as a ch-child you will pass on to people around you, from now on this house is going to be a house of happiness. From this evening on every single one of us is going to consider other people's feelings.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Georgia Byng: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a sabedoria. O trecho “Now, we all have stories of how we got here, and prob-probably some of you feel angry who whoever…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, abandonment, behavior pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de behavior e breaking-cycles.

frase_original: Now, we all have stories of how we got here, and prob-probably some of you feel angry who whoever it is who's left you here. But you must try and remember that they were like that because that's how they were taught to be. You m-must try to forgive them. Baby cuckoos can't unlearn their bad habits. But we should try to, and because what you learn as a ch-child you will pass on to people around you, from now on this house is going to be a house of happiness. From this evening on every single one of us is going to consider other people's feelings.
